lines.target: English people are very fond of sport.
lines.base: Los ingleses son muy aficionados al deporte.

lines.target: They play it and they watch it; they talk about it and think* about it.
lines.base: Lo juegan y lo contemplan; hablan de él y piensan en él.

lines.target: The most typically English game is cricket, which is played during the summer months.
lines.base: El juego más típicamente inglés es el cricket, que se juega durante los meses de verano.

lines.target: But the most popular game is football, which is played during the rest of the year (for eight months).
lines.base: Pero el deporte (juego) más popular es el fútbol, que se juega durante el resto del año (durante ocho meses).

lines.target: Professional football is very exciting to watch and the players earn large sums of money.
lines.base: El fútbol profesional es muy emocionante de ver y los jugadores ganan grandes sumas de dinero.

lines.target: Another ball game, less popular than football, is Rugby.
lines.base: Otro juego de pelota, menos popular que el fútbol, es el rugby.

lines.target: Called Rugby football, it was invented at Rugby School in about 1820 (eighteen twenty).
lines.base: Llamado rugby-fútbol, se inventó en la escuela de Rugby hacia el 1820.

lines.target: A boy, called Ellis, was so bored with playing with his feet,
lines.base: Un muchacho, llamado Ellis, estaba tan aburrido de jugar con los (sus) pies,

lines.target: that he took* the ball in his hands—and a new game was born!
lines.base: que cogió el balón con las (sus) manos — ¡y nació un nuevo juego!

lines.target: Another popular sport is horse racing, which is forbidden* in England on Sundays.
lines.base: Otro deporte popular son las carreras de caballos (singular) que están prohibidas en Inglaterra los domingos.

lines.target: There is no State lottery in England, but a game called Bingo is very successful.
lines.base: No hay lotería nacional en Inglaterra, pero tiene mucho éxito un juego llamado Bingo.

lines.target: Many cinemas are closing and being converted into Bingo halls.
lines.base: Muchos cines cierran (prog.) y se convierten en salas de Bingo.

lines.target: It is estimated that about six million people, mainly women, play Bingo regularly.
lines.base: Se cree que unos seis millones de personas, principalmente mujeres, juegan al Bingo regularmente.

gap_candidates.target: English people are very fond of sport. (1)
They play it and they watch it; they talk about it and think* about it. (2)
The most typically English game is cricket, which is played during the summer months.
But the most popular game is football, which is played during the rest of the year (for eight months).
Professional football is very exciting to watch and the players earn large sums of money.
Another ball game, less popular than football, is Rugby.
Called Rugby football, it was invented at Rugby School in about 1820 (eighteen twenty).
A boy, called Ellis, was so bored with playing with his feet,
that he took* the ball in his hands—and a new game was born! (3)
Another popular sport is horse racing, which is forbidden* in England on Sundays.
There is no State lottery in England, but a game called Bingo is very successful. (4) (5)
Many cinemas are closing and being converted into Bingo halls.
It is estimated that about six million people, mainly women, play Bingo regularly.
